# FAQ Template

> A reusable Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) template for educational websites, Government Recruitment portals, blogs, tutorials, and guides.
>
> Replace all placeholders (`{{ }}`) before publishing.

---

# Frequently Asked Questions

## About This Template

A good FAQ section helps users quickly find answers to common questions without reading the entire article.

It also improves content organization and complements the rest of the page.

Only include questions that are genuinely relevant to the topic.

# Writing Guidelines

Good answers should be:

- Accurate
- Concise
- Complete
- Easy to understand
- Based on official information where applicable

Avoid copying entire sections of official notifications when a clear summary is sufficient.

---

# SEO Guidelines

- [ ] Questions reflect real user searches.
- [ ] Answers are descriptive.
- [ ] Avoid duplicate questions.
- [ ] Keep terminology consistent.
- [ ] Update FAQs when information changes.

---

# GEO Guidelines

- [ ] Main entities identified.
- [ ] Context included where needed.
- [ ] Official sources referenced.
- [ ] Related resources linked.

---

# AEO Guidelines

- [ ] Primary question answered early.
- [ ] Answers remain concise.
- [ ] Questions use natural language.
- [ ] Information grouped logically.
- [ ] Headings clearly describe each question.

---

# FAQPage Structured Data

When appropriate, FAQPage structured data may be used to describe visible FAQ content.

Before implementation:

- [ ] Questions appear on the page.
- [ ] Answers match visible content.
- [ ] Structured data validated.
- [ ] Hidden FAQs avoided.

---

# Common Mistakes

Avoid:

- Duplicate questions.
- Placeholder answers.
- Keyword stuffing.
- Unsupported claims.
- Outdated information.
- Questions unrelated to the page.
- Hidden FAQ content.

---

# Publishing Checklist

Before publishing:

- [ ] Questions reviewed.
- [ ] Answers verified.
- [ ] Official links checked.
- [ ] Internal links added where useful.
- [ ] Structured data validated (if used).
- [ ] Proofreading completed.

# Disclaimer

This template is intended to help organize frequently asked questions in a clear and consistent manner.

Always verify answers against official or authoritative sources before publication.
